title: Three orthogonal validation assays for the headline mechanism
analysis_summary: Drafted a validation plan for the candidate's headline mechanism. The plan prioritizes orthogonal readouts over same-method replication: a cell-based functional assay, an in-vitro binding assay, and a transcriptomics readout in a tissue context. Each assay is independent of the candidate's existing pipeline so a positive result lands as genuinely new evidence rather than confirming the candidate's own framing.
findings: Proposed assays: (a) HSA-derived cell-line functional readout, ~14 days, ~$3K; (b) SPR binding at the target site, ~7 days, ~$4K; (c) bulk RNA-seq in matched tissue, ~21 days, ~$6K. Recommend running (a) and (b) in parallel, gating (c) on positive (a) signal. Total turnaround ~3 weeks with one go/no-go gate.
limitations: Cost bands are reasonable-case estimates from public vendor pricing, not negotiated quotes. The cell-line shortlist assumes HSA-derived lines are available; if not, the plan needs an alternate cell-line search step.
